ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Sambal Telur / Telur Balado. Mapped to the broad 'egg dish' class as the closest defensible FoodOn anchor. A more specific term for deep-fried egg in chili sambal sauce is absent from the ontology as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Egg-based dishes) is the closest defensible category. No specific FoodEx2 entry exists for Telur Balado or Sambal Telur as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps to Sambal Telur as a prepared dish. SNOMED CT is not applicable for this food record.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Sambal Telur is one deep-fried hard-boiled egg coated in sambal sauce, weighing approximately 100 g, as commonly served in Nasi Padang or Nasi Melayu mixed rice spreads.

nutrient_source.title: Nutrient Composition of Selected Malaysian Foods — IMR Malaysia MyFCD / HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (closest analogous entry: egg-based side dish with chili sambal)
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research (IMR) Malaysia; Health Promotion Board (HPB) Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Sambal Telur / Telur Balado as a composite dish. Values are adapted from IMR Malaysia MyFCD egg-based side dish entries and cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo data for hard-boiled egg and chili sambal components. Input AI-generated values (confidence 0.9) are retained where they align with the adapted source range. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 4.5 g per 100 g based on typical frying oil and egg yolk contribution per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (egg, hard-boiled, FDC ID 173424) and palm oil frying contribution.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Mixed Dishes and Egg-Based Foods — analogous estimate based on hard-boiled egg and chili sambal components
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Sambal Telur / Telur Balado in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on the dominant components: hard-boiled egg (GI ~0, negligible carbohydrate) and chili sambal (low-GI vegetables and spices). The high protein (9.5 g) and fat (16.5 g) content per 100 g substantially attenuate glycemic response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (35 × 6.8) / 100 = 2.38, rounded to 2.4. This is consistent with the low-GI classification. Estimation basis documented per Project Veritas v2 GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 380 mg per 100 g from sambal sauce and added salt
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Sambal Telur to small portions (≤80 g) and request reduced-salt sambal preparation, as a standard 100 g serving contributes approximately 16–19% of the 2000 mg daily sodium target recommended by the Singapore HPB and WHO.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~35) with high protein and fat moderating postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Sambal Telur is generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low GI (~35), high protein (9.5 g/100 g), and moderate carbohydrate content (6.8 g/100 g); however, the deep-frying process increases caloric density, so portion control to one egg per serving is advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated total fat (16.5 g/100 g) and saturated fat (~4.5 g/100 g) from deep-frying and egg yolk, combined with moderate sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume Sambal Telur in moderation; the deep-frying process increases saturated fat intake, and the sodium content (380 mg/100 g) may contribute to blood pressure elevation, so limiting to occasional consumption and choosing air-fried or shallow-fried preparation where possible is recommended.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Capsaicin in chilies has anti-inflammatory properties, but deep-frying may promote pro-inflammatory lipid oxidation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Capsaicin from the chili sambal may offer mild anti-inflammatory benefit, but deep-fried preparations can introduce oxidized lipids that are pro-inflammatory; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis may prefer a version prepared with minimal frying oil.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sambal Telur (also known as Telur Balado or Telur Belado) is a popular Malay and Minangkabau dish consisting of hard-boiled eggs that are deep-fried until the outer egg white turns golden and develops a slightly blistered, chewy texture. The fried eggs are then coated or tossed in a vibrant belado (balado) sambal sauce — a spicy, slightly sweet, and tangy chili paste made from ground red chilies, shallots, garlic, tomatoes, and salt, sautéed in oil. The dish is a staple in Nasi Padang and Nasi Melayu mixed rice spreads across Singapore, Malaysia, and Indonesia. Nutritionally, each 100 g serving provides approximately 210 kcal, 9.5 g protein, 16.5 g fat, and 6.8 g carbohydrate. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 35 (low), making it relatively blood-sugar friendly despite the frying process. Sodium content is moderate-to-high at 380 mg per 100 g, which is a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The dish is a good source of selenium (22 mcg), vitamin A (180 mcg RAE), riboflavin (0.3 mg), and vitamin B12 (0.7 mcg) from the egg component. It is naturally gluten-free and halal. The combination of protein and fat from the egg and frying oil results in high satiety per serving.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Sambal Telur / Telur Balado as a composite dish in IMR Malaysia MyFCD, HPB Singapore Foodo, or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from approved-source component data (egg-based side dishes, chili sambal) and cross-referenced with the AI-generated input values (confidence 0.9). GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component logic. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, cholesterol, and moisture data for Sambal Telur as a composite deep-fried egg and sambal dish are not available as a single entry in IMR Malaysia MyFCD, HPB Singapore Foodo, or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. Saturated fat (4.5 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy hard-boiled egg (FDC ID 173424) and typical palm oil frying contribution. 2. The input sources ('Malaysian Journal of Nutrition' at nutriweb.org.my and 'Southeast Asian Journal of Tropical Medicine and Public Health') are not on the Project Veritas v2 approved source list and have been replaced with IMR Malaysia MyFCD and HPB Singapore Foodo as primary approved sources, with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as secondary reference. 3. GI value of 35 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Sambal Telur exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2024. 4. Two portions in the input both had is_default: true; the canonical default serving is set to 100 g. 5. This dish contains significant carbohydrates (6.8 g/100 g) from the sambal sauce ingredients; the GI not-applicable rule for pure egg/fat foods does not apply here.
